summary: End-to-end testing of Dream's Latkans & Laces e-commerce site. Backend (6/6 pytest tests pass) and frontend (Home, Products, About, Contact + mobile) verified. All 25 AI images load, WhatsApp links use wa.me/919004356576, Google Maps embed renders, contact form validation + happy-path persistence working. No critical issues.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 on creation (not strictly wrong, but 201 is more idiomatic for resource creation).

issues: Three announcement items wrap to multiple lines and crowd above the navbar; consider hiding 1-2 items or converting to a marquee on small viewports.

action_items: (Optional) Return HTTP 201 from POST /api/enquiry for proper REST semantics.
(Optional) Improve announcement bar layout on mobile <=390px so items don't wrap into 3 lines on top of navbar.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py correctly excludes Mongo _id via projection in GET /api/enquiries and via Pydantic model in POST response. Good.
Pydantic EnquiryCreate enforces required fields; FastAPI returns 422 on missing fields as expected.
All frontend interactive elements have data-testid attributes. Note: nav links use 'nav-link-products' (not 'nav-products') — fine, consistent within Navbar.jsx.
GenImage falls back gracefully on error; all 25 images present in /app/frontend/public/images/.
seed_data_creation: Created 3 TEST_-prefixed enquiries via API + 1 via UI happy-path submission. Visible in GET /api/enquiries.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py covers health + enquiry CRUD/validation. Public URL: https://dreams-wholesale-1.preview.emergentagent.com. WhatsApp primary number 919004356576, alt 919820918646. Form submits via axios POST to ${REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api/enquiry. All 25 images served from frontend /public/images/. Mobile breakpoint at 1024px shows hamburger. Verified: hero 5-slide carousel (dots/arrows/auto), product filters (All=20, latkans=6, laces=4), about page images, contact form (validation + happy path + success state with WA button), Google Maps iframe with Bhuleshwar query, mobile nav.
